instruction:
Find the derivative of the 25th order $y^{(25)}$ for a function $y = 2 \cdot x^2 \cdot \sin(x)$.

Options:
A. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)+100\cdot x\cdot\cos(x) + 75\cdot\sin(x)
B. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)+100\cdot x\cdot\cos(x) - 25\cdot\sin(x)
C. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)+100\cdot x\cdot\cos(x) + 25\cdot\sin(x)
D. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)+100\cdot x\cdot\cos(x) + 50\cdot\sin(x)
E. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)+100\cdot x\cdot\cos(x)
F. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)+100\cdot x\cdot\sin(x)
G. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)-100\cdot x\cdot\cos(x)
H. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)-100\cdot x\cdot\sin(x) + 25\cdot\sin(x)
I. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)-100\cdot x\cdot\sin(x)
J. \(2\cdot x^2-1200\)\cdot\cos(x)+100\cdot x\cdot\cos(x) - 50\cdot\sin(x)
